tools.name: golangci-lint
tools.scope: maas-api module only (maas-controller has no .golangci.yml config)
tools.notes: Linter is auto-installed by Makefile. Uses 'all' linters with specific exclusions. Includes formatters: gci, gofmt, goimports.

linting.notes: maas-api has comprehensive golangci-lint config. maas-controller uses same tooling but no custom config file (relies on defaults). The make lint target runs both formatting and linting.

testing.test_directory: Tests distributed across modules: maas-api/cmd/*_test.go, maas-api/internal/*/*_test.go, maas-controller/pkg/controller/maas/*_test.go
testing.run_command: cd maas-api && make test && cd ../maas-controller && make test
testing.mock_patterns: Tests use standard Go testing with table-driven tests. Controller tests use controller-runtime test framework.
testing.notes: maas-api uses -race flag and generates coverage reports (coverage.out, coverage.html). maas-controller runs simpler 'go test ./...' without coverage. Tests require no external infrastructure - all unit tests.

gating_checks.name: verify-codegen (maas-controller)
gating_checks.trigger: pull_request with changes to maas-controller/api/**, deployment/base/maas-controller/crd/**

gating_checks.name: validate-manifests
gating_checks.trigger: pull_request with changes to deployment/** or maas-controller/api/**

ci.notes: CI uses GitHub Actions for PR validation. Tekton pipelines handle container image builds for deployment. Go version is read from go.mod. golangci-lint version is extracted from tools.mk. Tests upload coverage artifacts for maas-api.

build.install_command: cd maas-api && go mod download && cd ../maas-controller && go mod download
build.build_command: cd maas-api && make binary && cd ../maas-controller && make build
build.container_build_command: cd maas-api && make build-image && cd ../maas-controller && make build-image
build.notes: Two separate Go modules. Uses Go 1.25. Container images use registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/go-toolset:1.25 for build and ubi9/ubi-minimal for runtime. Supports FIPS via GO_STRICTFIPS=true.

conventions.test_naming: Functions named Test* for standard tests. Uses table-driven test pattern extensively.
conventions.import_style: gci formatter enforces: standard, default, prefix(github.com/opendatahub-io/models-as-a-service/maas-api/), blank, dot
conventions.notes: Code follows standard Go conventions. Uses controller-runtime patterns for Kubernetes controllers. API types in maas-controller/api/maas/v1alpha1.

lint_commands.notes: Passed with 0 issues. Auto-installs golangci-lint v2.6.2.
Failed with 4 lint issues: 2 staticcheck (should convert type instead of struct literal), 2 unused functions. Also has formatting issues (missing/extra newlines).

test_commands.notes: Passed. All tests passed with 84.7% coverage. Generates coverage.out and coverage.html.
Passed. Tests in pkg/controller/maas completed successfully.

container_recipe.notes: Container needs read-write access to /app for test artifacts (coverage.out, coverage.html, bin/ directories). Both modules can be tested independently.

golangci-lint fmt --diff
diff pkg/controller/maas/maasauthpolicy_controller_test.go.orig...
make: *** [Makefile:80: lint] Error 1
results.notes: Linting found formatting issues (newlines) and 4 code issues (2 staticcheck, 2 unused functions)

results.output_snippet:
go test -v -race -coverprofile=coverage.out /app/maas-api/cmd/... /app/maas-api/internal/...
...
PASS
coverage: 84.7% of statements
GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 CGO_ENABLED=1 go build -ldflags "-s -w -X main.version=latest -X main.commit=17deedb -X main.buildTime=2026-03-22_23:28:09" -o /app/maas-api/bin/maas-api ./cmd/

validation.summary: install OK (both modules), lint OK (maas-api), lint FAILED (maas-controller: 4 issues + formatting), tests OK (both modules), build OK (both modules)

gaps: maas-controller has no .golangci.yml config file - lint target uses golangci-lint without custom configuration
maas-controller lint currently fails with 4 issues (2 staticcheck, 2 unused functions) and formatting violations
maas-controller does not generate test coverage reports (unlike maas-api)
No integration tests found - all tests are unit tests
Tests do not require external services (PostgreSQL, Kubernetes cluster) - good for CI but may not catch integration issues
agent_readiness_rationale: maas-api is ready for automated validation: lint and tests both pass. maas-controller has lint failures that need fixing before it can be used for automated validation. However, tests pass for both modules, so partial validation is possible.
